title: Ensure TLS 1.2 for all inbound traffic at IBM Cloud  Kubernetes Service Ingress

input_row.title: Ensure TLS 1.2 for all inbound traffic at IBM Cloud  Kubernetes Service Ingress
input_row.description: Ensure that all insecure (HTTP) client requests to applications and services hosted on IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service are redirected to secure TLS connections (HTTPS), and ensure that only TLS versions 1.2+ are supported. You set up your Ingress ALB to secure your domain with the IBM-provided TLS certificate or your custom TLS certificate. Some users might try to access your apps by using an insecure http request to your ALB domain, for example http://www.myingress.com, instead of using https. You can use the redirect annotation to always convert insecure HTTP requests to HTTPS.
input_row.rationale: HTTPS uses the TLS protocol to provide a secure, encrypted connection between the client and the server. This protocol secures incoming requests to protect users from attack, for example, man in the middle attacks. IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service allows HTTPS to be configured, and it is highly recommended to do so. If you do not use this annotation, insecure HTTP requests are not converted into HTTPS requests by default and might expose unencrypted confidential information to the public.
input_row.audit: 1. Get the configuration for the ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm configmap resource. kubectl get cm ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm -n kube-system -o yaml 2. In the data section, ensure that the ssl-protocols key is not present, or if it is present, ensure that it does not include TLSv1 or TLSv1.1. 3. Get the configuration for your Ingress resource. by running the following command: kubectl get ingress -n -o yaml 4. In the annotations section, ensure that the ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to- https key is set to "True". apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 kind: Ingress metadata: name: myingress annotations: ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to-https: "True" spec: tls: - hosts: - mydomain secretName: mytlssecret rules: - host: mydomain http: paths: - path: / backend: serviceName: myservice servicePort: 8080
input_row.remediation: 1. Edit the configuration file for the ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm configmap resource by running the following command: kubectl edit cm ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm -n kube-system 2. In the data section, remove the ssl-protocols key, or remove TLSv1 and TLSv1.1 from the value. 3. Edit the configuration for your Ingress resource by running the following command: kubectl edit ingress -n 4. In the annotations section, add ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to-https: "True". apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 kind: Ingress metadata: name: myingress annotations: ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to-https: "True" spec: tls: - hosts: - mydomain secretName: mytlssecret rules: - host: mydomain http: paths: - path: / backend: serviceName: myservice servicePort: 8080
input_row.impact: Incoming requests will not be able to use HTTP because all incoming requests will be redirected to HTTPS. Legacy clients that require TLS 1.0 or 1.1 support will not be able to access your apps.
input_row.default_value: The default setting supports only TLS 1.2 and 1.3. Redirecting HTTP requests to HTTPS is disabled by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The audit can be fully verified through the IBM Cloud CLI as specific commands are outlined in the audit section and it is clear that these configurations can be accessed programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_ingress_tls_configuration_minimum_version

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

1. **IBM Cloud API calls or IBM Cloud CLI SDK methods to use:**
   - Use `kubectl` commands to retrieve the configuration of the Ingress resources and associated ConfigMap.

2. **IBM Cloud CLI commands (if applicable):**
   - Command to retrieve the ConfigMap: 
     ``` 
     kubectl get cm ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm -n kube-system -o yaml 
     ```
   - Command to retrieve the Ingress resource configuration:
     ``` 
     kubectl get ingress -n <namespace> -o yaml 
     ```

3. **Specific resources to enumerate:**
   - The `ibm-cloud-provider-ingress-cm` ConfigMap in the `kube-system` namespace.
   - All Ingress resources within specified namespaces.

4. **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
   - Verify that the `ssl-protocols` key is either not present or does not include `TLSv1` or `TLSv1.1` in the ConfigMap.
   - Check that the annotation `ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to-https` in the Ingress resource is set to `"True"`.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
   - **Pass:** `ssl-protocols` key is absent or explicitly excludes `TLSv1` and `TLSv1.1`. The annotation `ingress.bluemix.net/redirect-to-https` is present and set to `"True"`.
   - **Fail:** If `ssl-protocols` include `TLSv1` or `TLSv1.1` or the required annotation is missing or not set to `"True"`.

By automating this process, compliance with security best practices regarding TLS configurations for inbound traffic in IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service can be effectively evaluated.
